title: NotificationPreference
description: Per-user notification controls: which tiers may push, digest cadence, quiet hours, and per-source mute (FR-INB-006). Provided so other surfaces (Settings, Home) can read/render the user's controls. NON-NEGOTIABLE invariant: a Critical SAFETY alert is ALWAYS at least surfaced in-app and any push-suppression is audited — preferences can suppress a push but NEVER silently drop a safety alert (Clarifications Q5).

profile_id.description: Owning user; reads/writes authZ-checked server-side on session identity.

tier_push.description: Whether each tier may send a push. Informational defaults to in-app only (no push).

cadence.description: Digest delivery cadence. Default evening-only with morning user-enabled (Clarifications Q1). 'both' still respects the ≤2 money pushes/day budget (SC-009).

quiet_hours.description: Local-time window in which non-critical pushes are deferred to the next allowed window. A critical breakthrough still delivers but respects the critical ceiling.

muted_sources.description: module_ids muted from push. A muted source's NON-critical alerts are push-suppressed and shown muted in-app; its CRITICAL safety alerts are still surfaced in-app (non_suppressible_safety).

critical_ceiling.description: Max critical breakthrough pushes/day; excess coalesced (Clarifications Q2).

non_suppressible_safety.description: INVARIANT — always true. A Critical safety alert is always at least surfaced in-app regardless of any preference; push-suppression of a critical alert is recorded in the audit trail. This field cannot be set false (FR-INB-006, Clarifications Q5).

updated_at.description: UTC instant of last preference change (audited).
